title: viola arvensis subsp. trimestris
intro: Viola arvensis subsp. trimestris, also known as the Woodland Pansy or Field Pansy, is a subspecies of perennial flowering plant in the violet family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Viola arvensis subsp. trimestris include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
